hαSyn seeds (i.e. sonicated pre-formed) and fibrils (35 and 70 µM) / hIAPP monomers (2 μM) 

Together, these data provide evidence that hαSyn and hIAPP monomers dose-dependently and bi-directionally cross-seed and co-aggregate into hybrid amyloid fibrils at concentrations of αSyn monomer of 7 μM or higher, whereas seeds and fibrils are less potent at crossseeding IAPP monomer fibril formation and growth at equimolar concentrations.

αS fibrils as interactor, Aβ fibrils as interactee. The authors focused on studying inhibition, not accelerated aggregation. Therefore, they do not conclude that accelerated aggregation occurred as a result of the interaction of αS fibrils and Aβ fibrils. I can see this on the ThT charts. The authors also investigate the effect of αS monomers / αS oligomers on Aβ fibrils. However, as  the authors' interpretation is not clear and it is difficult to read the kinetics from the ThT plots, I did not enter these interactions into the questionnaire.

fibrillar forms of α-synuclein can also induce tau aggregation.

two proteins can be extended to understand synergistic
